title: Spiesia chiliophylla
taxonomy: Spiesia chiliophylla is classified within the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as legumes. Further, it belongs to the subfamily Faboideae, a subfamily within the Fabaceae family that includes many species of peas, beans, and other related plants. Within the subfamily Faboideae, Spiesia chiliophylla is placed in the tribe Millinae, which includes species of vetches and other related plants. The genus Spiesia is a small genus within the tribe Millinae, and Spiesia chiliophylla is one of the species within this genus. Overall, the classification of Spiesia chiliophylla reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Fabaceae family.

intro: Spiesia chiliophylla, also known as Spilanthes or "toothache plant," is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and alleviate pain. The bioactive constituents of Spiesia chiliophylla include spilanthol, a sesquiterpene that has been shown to have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. Spiesia chiliophylla is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often marketed as natural remedies for mouth sores, toothaches, and other oral health issues.
